verification.note: YOLOv6-M (L): RadDet (Huang, Denman, Pemasiri, Martin, Fookes, ICASSP 2025, arXiv:2501.10407), Table III, column RadDet-9T @512x512 (the DENSE SYNTHETIC variant at 512 px). NOT reproduced/re-run by this repo. Our canonical split detect-raddet-detection-official-v1 ADOPTS RadDet's own published train/val/test partition verbatim (images/{{train,val,test}}/ from the archive; our test = their test set), so this figure is directly comparable -> 'from_paper' (not uncertain). Board primary 'mAP' = COCO AP@[0.50:0.95] (= the paper's mAP50:95); mAP50 kept as a secondary value. This is the SYNTHETIC RadDet-9T variant we hold on the cluster, NOT the NIST-CBRS real captures that back the paper's headline RT-DETR-L 95.31 mAP50. Verification: the YOLOv3-L (53.97) and YOLOv9-M (40.85) mAP50:95 cells were re-read verbatim from the PDF via pdftotext in the 2026-07-22 sweep; the full 512/9T column (incl. YOLOv6-M, RT-DETR-L) was cross-read from the arXiv HTML Table III, agreeing exactly on the two pdftotext-verified cells. Literature reference point, never a repro.
